response.justification: Bogdan is a seasoned full-stack engineer with 15+ years of broad software development experience and genuine staff-level seniority, but his AI/GenAI work is confined to a six-month stint at re:cinq ending in late 2025, which is far short of the eight years of production AI delivery the role demands. Python — the dominant language in the AI/ML ecosystem and explicitly listed as a key technology — does not appear anywhere in his resume. Critical competencies such as vector databases, RAG architectures, LLM evaluation pipelines, and multi-agent frameworks (LangGraph, CrewAI, etc.) are entirely absent. His MCP tooling contribution at re:cinq is directionally relevant but insufficient on its own to bridge these gaps for a staff-level, customer-facing GenAI role.
strengths: Demonstrated staff-level engineering maturity across multiple organizations with evidence of technical leadership, mentoring, and cross-functional impact
Hands-on experience with MCP (Model Context Protocol) code generators and agentic workflow infrastructure at re:cinq, directly relevant to the role's tooling landscape
Strong TypeScript/Node.js foundation with AWS CDK exposure and a proven track record shipping production systems at scale (Dunnhumby: millions of HTTP req/s; Optilyz: millions of mailings)
concerns: Python is absent from his listed skills and work history, despite being the primary language for AI/ML development and explicitly required by the job description
No evidence of vector databases, RAG architectures, LLM evaluation/observability pipelines, or multi-agent frameworks — all of which are core to the role's day-to-day responsibilities
AI/GenAI experience totals roughly six months (re:cinq, Apr–Oct 2025) versus the eight-year minimum required; the bulk of his career is in unrelated domains (direct mail, GIS, embedded, adtech)
